# REPORT STANDARDS

## Report Format
- Professional SEO consulting report
- Enterprise-grade presentation
- Markdown source optimized for PDF generation
- Consistent heading hierarchy
- Numbered sections and subsections
- Professional tables throughout the report

## Writing Standards
- Use concise, professional language.
- Base findings on evidence whenever possible.
- Never invent website data.
- If evidence is unavailable, clearly mark it as "Not Detected", "Not Available", or "Requires Manual Verification".
- Every issue should include a recommendation.
- Every recommendation should include business value when applicable.

## Severity Levels
- Critical
- High
- Medium
- Low
- Informational

## Table Standards
- Use Markdown tables whenever structured data is presented.
- Maintain consistent column names throughout the report.
- Keep similar tables structurally identical across sections.

## Recommendations
Every recommendation should include:
- Recommendation
- Priority
- Expected SEO Impact
- Estimated Implementation Effort

## Scoring
Use a score from 0–100 for:
- Overall SEO
- Technical SEO
- On-Page SEO
- Content SEO
- Off-Page SEO
- Local SEO
- AI Search Readiness

## Evidence
Every finding should include at least one of:
- Crawled URL
- HTML element
- Metadata
- Screenshot reference
- Tool output
- Manual verification note

## Placeholders
Dynamic values should use:
{{placeholder_name}}

Example:
{{website_url}}
{{overall_score}}
{{page_title}}
{{recommendation}}

# PART 12: APPENDIX & REPORT INFORMATION

## 12.1 Glossary

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| AI Search | Search experiences powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI assistants. |
| Alt Text | Alternative text describing an image for accessibility and SEO. |
| Backlink | A hyperlink from another website pointing to this website. |
| Canonical URL | The preferred version of a webpage used to prevent duplicate content issues. |
| Core Web Vitals | Google's user experience metrics including LCP, INP/FID, and CLS. |
| Crawlability | The ability of search engine bots to discover website pages. |
| E-E-A-T | Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness and Trustworthiness. |
| GEO | Generative Engine Optimization for AI-powered search systems. |
| Indexability | The ability of a page to be indexed by search engines. |
| Internal Link | A hyperlink connecting two pages within the same website. |
| Keyword Cannibalization | Multiple pages competing for the same search query. |
| LLM | Large Language Model used by AI search platforms. |
| Meta Description | HTML description shown in search results. |
| Organic Traffic | Visitors arriving through unpaid search engine results. |
| Schema Markup | Structured data that helps search engines understand page content. |
| SERP | Search Engine Results Page. |
| Sitemap | XML file listing pages that should be crawled by search engines. |
| Structured Data | Standardized markup describing website content. |
| Topical Authority | Overall expertise demonstrated on a specific subject. |

---

## 12.2 Scoring Methodology

The SEO scores presented throughout this report are calculated using a weighted evaluation model that considers multiple SEO disciplines.

The final overall score is derived from:

- Technical SEO
- On-Page SEO
- Content Strategy
- Keyword Strategy
- Off-Page SEO
- AI Search & GEO
- User Experience
- Local SEO (if applicable)

Scores are intended to provide an overall health assessment and should be interpreted together with the detailed findings and recommendations contained within this report.

---

## 12.3 Report Methodology

This report is generated using a structured multi-stage SEO analysis process.

Typical workflow:

1. Website discovery and crawl
2. Technical SEO analysis
3. On-page SEO evaluation
4. Content assessment
5. Keyword analysis
6. Competitor analysis
7. Off-page authority assessment
8. AI Search (GEO) evaluation
9. Executive scoring
10. Recommendation generation

Each stage contributes structured data that is consolidated into the final report.

---

## 12.4 Data Sources

Depending on the available integrations and data sources, this report may use information from:

- Website crawl
- HTML source code
- XML sitemaps
- Robots.txt
- Structured data (Schema.org)
- Search engine index information
- Performance metrics
- Backlink data
- Keyword research tools
- Competitor analysis
- Publicly available website information
- AI-generated analysis

---

## 12.5 Assumptions

The findings and recommendations in this report are based on the data available at the time of analysis.

Results may vary depending on:

- Search engine algorithm updates
- Website changes after the audit
- Availability of external data
- Market competition
- Industry trends
- Geographic targeting
- Personalization of search results

---

## 12.6 Limitations

This report should be considered an advisory document.

Certain findings may be limited by:

- Restricted website access
- Authentication-protected content
- JavaScript rendering limitations
- Third-party API availability
- Rate limits
- Dynamic website behavior
- Incomplete search engine data

---

## 12.7 Recommended Review Frequency

| Report Type | Recommended Frequency |
|-------------|----------------------|
| Technical SEO Audit | Monthly |
| On-Page SEO Audit | Quarterly |
| Content Audit | Quarterly |
| Keyword Strategy | Quarterly |
| Competitor Analysis | Quarterly |
| Off-Page SEO Review | Monthly |
| AI Search (GEO) Review | Monthly |
| Full Enterprise SEO Audit | Every 6–12 Months |

---

## 12.8 Recommended SEO Tool Categories

Typical categories used during enterprise SEO analysis include:

- Website Crawlers
- Search Performance Platforms
- Keyword Research Platforms
- Technical SEO Validators
- Structured Data Validators
- Performance Testing Tools
- Accessibility Testing Tools

- AI Search Monitoring Platforms
- Analytics Platforms

## 12.11 Disclaimer

This report is intended to provide professional SEO recommendations based on the available information at the time of analysis.

SEO performance is influenced by numerous factors, including search engine algorithm updates, market competition, website implementation quality, user behavior, and external signals. Consequently, rankings, traffic, and business outcomes cannot be guaranteed.

All recommendations should be reviewed, validated, and implemented according to the organization's technical, legal, and business requirements.
